Attorney at Law, Associate Director, Graduate Admissions, Mendoza College of Business

Member at [Black Executive Men Capital Network](https://www.linkedin.com/company/blackexecutivemencapital)

San Francisco Bay Area (US)

500 connections • 1,911 followers

## About

In today's complex financial landscape, navigating life-changing events and planning for a secure retirement demand more than just investment advice. As a seasoned professional with a robust legal background, I specialize in delivering comprehensive financial and tax planning services designed to simplify your financial life and ensure a future you can look forward to with confidence. Why Our Services Stand Out: Expert Delegation: Our clients enjoy peace of mind by entrusting their financial management to an expert, freeing them to relish their lives and retirement. Certainty of Outcome: We prioritize achieving your financial objectives over chasing market trends, ensuring a reliable path to your desired lifestyle in retirement. Strategic Financial Planning: Our services encompass tailored financial planning, retirement income strategies, and meticulous investment management, with a keen focus on legal and tax considerations to optimize your financial well-being. Our Approach: We believe in building your financial plan on the foundation of what matters most to you: Values: Align your financial strategies with your values. Goals: Setting clear, achievable goals for a fulfilling retirement. Results: Focused actions to reach your desired outcomes, ensuring your financial security. Our Commitment to Excellence: With a passion for helping our clients achieve their financial aspirations, we specialize in creating robust plans for pre-retirees and retirees. Our approach integrates your household balance sheet into a comprehensive strategy, emphasizing tax efficiency, risk management, and sustainable income to secure your lifestyle against the uncertainties of the future, the economy, and market fluctuations. The Benefits of Partnering with Us: Organization Accountability Objectivity Proactive Planning Education Partnership Our Promise: As fiduciaries, we are committed to acting in your best interest at all times. We do not sell products; our sole compensation is fee-only, directly from our clients. This ensures our advice is transparent, unbiased, and tailored to your unique situation. Let's embark on a journey to secure your financial future, leveraging my legal expertise and tax

The Case for Independence: Why Wirehouse Advisors Are Leaving

Agree & Join LinkedIn

By clicking Continue to join or sign in, you agree to LinkedIn’s User Agreement, Privacy Policy, and Cookie Policy.

For a long time, the traditional wirehouse model has been the standard for financial advisors, providing brand recognition, infrastructure, and established clients. However, over the past decade, an increasing number of advisors are reevaluating whether this model genuinely meets their clients' needs, their business objectives, or their long-term financial aspirations.

The trend is clearly shifting towards independence, and for valid reasons. The advisor headcount in the wirehouse channel has declined at a CAGR of -1.3% from 2012 to 2022. In contrast, the independent RIA channel has seen a CAGR of 2.4% over the last ten years.

Between 2018 and 2023, the RIA's market share increased from about 22% to over 30% of advised assets, while the wirehouse channel's market share has consistently decreased over the past decade—from more than 40% in the early 2010s to approximately 32–34% in recent years, according to public summaries of Cerulli data.

This post delves into the main reasons advisors are transitioning from wirehouses to independent models and the advantages they achieve.

### The Financial Upside: Income & Equity Growth

One of the primary reasons advisors choose independence is the significant boost in income potential. In a wirehouse, advisors generally keep 25-50% of their revenue. On the other hand, independent advisors can retain over 90% of their gross revenue, allowing them to reinvest in their business.

Consider the following scenario:

- Advisor transitions to independence and now grosses the entire $2 million to themself.
- Wirehouse provided a 40% payout, the advisor's net revenue amounted to $800,000, which is a small percentage of the advisor's total revenue of $2 million.
- Advisors Transitions to RIA/Fee model charging 1% on the existing $200 million Fee Only business (Not including TBP)
- Total revenue is $2 million ($1.5 million from fee-based revenue and $500,000 from transaction revenue (TBP).
- Wirehouse Advisor manages $250 million AUM ($200 million fee-based and $50 million transaction-based)

Blue = compensation at the Wirehouse, Green RIA

title: The Case for Independence: Why Wirehouse Advisors Are Leaving
